one_liner: LLY tagged a Sign of Strength (SOS) by the Wyckoff engine — 7.13% on 2.35x volume blasting through 90-day resistance at $1,170 — Phase C-D accumulation is now confirmed with 4/5 signals and DCF intrinsic value of $1,980.

price_action.intraday_story: LLY gapped up to open at $1,128.80 — exactly at Thursday's resistance level — then broke out hard through the day, touching $1,215.76 intraday before closing at $1,208.12, right near the session high. The close in the upper 95% of the day's range on 2.35x average volume is a textbook accumulation candle: strong effort matched by strong result. This is the exact intraday structure you want to see after a 6-week basing period.

news_catalyst.headline: Eli Lilly and BioArctic AB Enter Research and Collaboration Agreement; EMA Backing Secured for Jaypirca in CLL
news_catalyst.summary: Two simultaneous catalysts converged on LLY: the EMA's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) recommended approval of Jaypirca (pirtobrutinib) for chronic lymphocytic leukemia in Europe, materially expanding the oncology revenue base beyond GLP-1; and a new research collaboration agreement with BioArctic AB for a next-generation Alzheimer's treatment. The BioArctic deal signals Lilly is extending its moat into neurodegeneration — a $100B+ untapped market — while the Jaypirca approval adds a near-term, de-risked revenue stream in CLL, where BTK inhibitors command premium pricing. The timing matters: the market is rotating into healthcare names (per IBD's 'market at tipping point' coverage), and LLY was already the cleanest setup in the watchlist heading into these announcements. The 7.1% single-day move is proportionate to the combined signal strength.

technical.summary:
The Wyckoff engine formally tagged this session as a Sign of Strength (SOS) — the highest-conviction accumulation signal in the framework. At 2.35x average volume and the second-highest volume day in the 90-day dataset, the effort and result are in harmony on the bullish side: massive institutional buying drove price through the $1,170 resistance and closed near the session high. This is not absorption (which is high effort, muted result) — this is markup.

Zooming out: LLY had been consolidating between roughly $1,058 (BB lower) and $1,185 (BB upper) for several weeks after a prior markup phase. The breakout today clears the Bollinger Band upper at $1,185 and closes above it at $1,208 — a BB squeeze breakout on institutional volume. RSI at 72.7 is elevated but not yet signaling reversal; in a confirmed markup phase, RSI can sustain 70+ for multiple sessions.

For confirmation over the next 2-3 sessions: what Wyckoff calls the 'Back-Up to the Edge of the Creek' (BUEC) would look like the stock pulling back to $1,170-1,185 on below-average volume (drying volume = healthy test), then resuming higher. A close below $1,128 on elevated volume would be the invalidation signal — it would suggest today was an upthrust rather than a genuine SOS.

wyckoff_narrative:
The Wyckoff structure on LLY is the clearest in the entire watchlist. The dataset shows 4 of 5 accumulation signals confirmed (SC, AR, ST, and now SOS), placing the stock firmly in Phase C-D — the transition from accumulation to markup. The SOS tag today is algorithmically assigned by the backend (wyckoff_events: ['SOS']), confirming this isn't a subjective read.

The sequence: a selling climax established the base, an automatic rally followed, a secondary test retested the SC lows on lower volume (confirming smart money absorbed the supply), and today's SOS represents the first major show of demand in the markup direction — price breaking out of the trading range with authority on above-average volume. The fifth and final expected signal would be a Last Point of Support (LPS) — a low-volume pullback above the prior resistance at $1,170, ideally holding for 1-3 sessions before the next leg up.

The only note of caution: with RSI at 72.7, a short-term overbought condition exists. But in Wyckoff Phase C-D, you buy pullbacks, not tops — the structural signal overrides the oscillator signal. Position sizing should reflect the 64% DCF upside and a stop below $1,128 (today's open = the invalidation level).

valuation.summary: DCF base case of $1,980 implies 64% upside from current price, driven by a 36% projected FCF growth rate reflecting GLP-1 market leadership and oncology pipeline expansion. The bear case at $941 is 22% below current price — asymmetric risk/reward of roughly 3:1. Current price of $1,208 represents a genuine margin of safety against the base case, even adjusting for the 7% single-day move.

key_strengths: 36% analyst-estimated FCF growth rate — top-tier for any S&P 500 company
GLP-1 market leadership (tirzepatide/Mounjaro) with multi-year revenue visibility
EMA approval of Jaypirca adds de-risked oncology revenue stream
key_concerns: RSI at 72.7 — short-term overbought, pullback likely before next leg
Pipeline binary risk remains (clinical failures can gap stock 20%+)
fundamentals.summary: Grade B reflects strong growth and business quality offset by premium valuation risk. The 36% growth rate is genuine — it reflects analyst consensus on the GLP-1 cycle, not speculation. Today's BioArctic deal broadens the pipeline beyond metabolic disease, reducing single-indication concentration risk. Fundamentals clearly support the technical accumulate stance.

risks: RSI at 72.7 and close near session high — expect a 2-5% pullback before continuation; if the pullback holds above $1,170 on low volume it's a buy; if it closes below $1,128 on elevated volume the SOS is invalidated
Pipeline binary risk: a Phase 3 failure on any major asset (donanemab, retatrutide) could gap the stock 15-25% regardless of GLP-1 performance
GLP-1 competitive pressure from Novo Nordisk's semaglutide pipeline and emerging oral GLP-1 competitors could compress long-term growth rate below the 36% assumption
